sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Toyota (TM) Recalls 4,400 Lexus LX 600 Vehicles Over Transmission Software Issue
feed.summary: Toyota (TM) has issued a safety recall for approximately 4,400 Lexus LX 600 vehicles from the 2025-2026 models due to a transmission software glitch that could cause power loss, accident risk, and fire hazard. Lexus dealers will perform a complimentary software update, and owners will be notified by the end of April 2026. Despite this operational challenge, Toyota maintains robust profitability and market position, though concerns about financial strength and valuation remain.

feed.title: Nordson earnings matched, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Nordson (NASDAQ: NDSN) reported first-quarter earnings per share that matched analyst estimates and revenue that exceeded expectations. The company also provided optimistic guidance for Q2 and the full fiscal year 2026, with revenue and EPS projections generally above current analyst consensus. Nordson's stock has shown strong performance over the last 3 and 12 months, with a "good performance" financial health score from InvestingPro.

feed.title: Regency Centers Prices $450 Million Senior Unsecured Notes Offering
feed.summary: Regency Centers Corporation announced that its operating partnership has priced a $450 million public offering of senior unsecured notes due 2033. The notes, with a 4.50% coupon, were issued at 99.376% of par value. Regency intends to use the net proceeds to reduce its line of credit, repay existing 3.81% notes due May 2026, and for general corporate purposes including capital expenditures and future debt repayment.

feed.title: American Water Works Co 2025 adjusted EPS rises
feed.summary: American Water Works Co (AWK) is projected to see a rise in its adjusted earnings per share (EPS) for the year 2025. This brief report, sourced from Refinitiv via Reuters, indicates positive financial growth for the company.

feed.title: PYPL Stockholder Alert: Shareholder Rights Law Firm Robbins LLP Reminds Investors of the Class Action Lawsuit Against PayPal Holdings, Inc.
feed.summary: Robbins LLP is reminding investors of a class action lawsuit filed against PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL). The lawsuit alleges that PayPal misled investors regarding its projected revenue growth and downplayed risks, particularly concerning its Branded Checkout offerings. Following disappointing Q4 2025 and full-year financial results and news of a CEO change, PayPal's stock price dropped by over 20% in one day.

feed.title: MOODYS CORP /DE/ SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Moody's Corporation has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing robust financial performance in the fiscal year with total revenue of $7,718 million and net income of $2,459 million. The report highlights strong growth in both its Analytics and Investors Service segments, driven by strategic initiatives, new product launches, and global expansion, while also acknowledging significant challenges related to regulatory, legal, operational, and market risks. The company remains committed to capital management through dividends and share repurchases, and is focusing on technology integration and sustainability initiatives.

feed.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT (NYSE:ARR) Posts Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.03 EPS
feed.summary: ARMOUR Residential REIT (NYSE:ARR) recently announced its quarterly earnings, reporting $0.71 EPS, which missed analyst consensus estimates by $0.03. The company generated $236.50 million in revenue for the quarter and also declared a monthly dividend of $0.24 per share, payable on March 30th to shareholders of record on March 16th.

feed.title: Equinix (EQIX) Announces Transition of Chief Sales Officer
feed.summary: Equinix (EQIX) has announced that its Chief Sales Officer, Mike Campbell, will retire on March 31, 2026, and transition into a part-time Special Advisor role until March 5, 2027. During this transition period, Campbell will assist with duties and provide strategic advice to the Chief Customer and Revenue Officer, receiving an annual base salary of $150,000 without bonus eligibility. This change in leadership structure is expected to draw investor attention regarding its impact on the company's operations and market performance.

feed.title: AMERICAN WATER REPORTS STRONG 2025 RESULTS; AFFIRMS 2026 EPS GUIDANCE AND LONG-TERM TARGETS
feed.summary: American Water Works Company, Inc. reported strong adjusted earnings of $5.64 per share for 2025, an 8.9% increase from 2024, and affirmed its 2026 adjusted EPS guidance of $6.02 to $6.12. The company also reaffirmed its long-term EPS and dividend growth targets of 7-9%. These positive results were driven by significant infrastructure investments and successful rate case outcomes, with shareholders overwhelmingly approving the merger-related proposals with Essential Utilities.

feed.title: Southern California Edison Strengthens Support for Impacted Altadena Community Members
feed.summary: Southern California Edison (SCE) has announced enhancements to its voluntary Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program, effective immediately and applied retroactively. These updates increase compensation for legal expenses to 20% of net damages and provide enhanced support for displaced tenants, covering up to three months of housing assistance. The program aims to provide prompt and certain relief to eligible individuals and businesses affected by the Eaton Fire, with 2,405 claims submitted and over $183 million in offers extended as of February 18, 2026.

feed.title: (INVH) Invitation Homes Expects 2026 Adjusted FFO Range $1.60 - $1.68, vs. FactSet Est of $1.68
feed.summary: Invitation Homes (INVH) anticipates its 2026 Adjusted Funds From Operations (FFO) to be in the range of $1.60 to $1.68 per share. This forecast aligns with FactSet's estimate of $1.68 per share. The announcement was made on February 18, 2026, by MT Newswires.

feed.title: Costco sued over rotisserie chicken food safety
feed.summary: A Missouri woman has filed a class-action lawsuit against Costco, alleging that its rotisserie chickens produced by Lincoln Premium Poultry have elevated Salmonella contamination rates. The lawsuit claims that Costco's vertically integrated poultry operation has repeatedly failed federal Salmonella performance standards since 2019, with the Lincoln Premium Poultry plant classified as Category 3 for exceeding allowable Salmonella thresholds in about 92% of reporting months. The plaintiff, Lisa Taylor, is seeking damages not to exceed $25,000 for herself and each class member.

feed.title: Nordson Corp. (NDSN) Reports In-Line Q1 EPS; offers guidance
feed.summary: Nordson Corp. (NDSN) reported Q1 EPS of $2.37, matching analyst estimates, and exceeded revenue consensus with $669 million. The company provided Q2 2026 guidance, expecting EPS between $2.70-$2.90 and revenue of $710-740 billion, generally in line with analyst expectations. For the full fiscal year 2026, Nordson projects EPS of $11.00-$11.60 and revenue of $2.86-2.98 billion, also aligning with consensus forecasts.

feed.title: Truist announces redemption of senior notes due March 2027
feed.summary: Truist Financial Corporation announced its plan to redeem all $1.25 billion of its fixed-to-floating rate senior notes due March 2, 2027, on March 2, 2026. The redemption will be at 100% of the principal amount plus accrued and unpaid interest, with interest ceasing on the redemption date. Truist is a leading financial services company headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, with $548 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: $1.25B Truist bond payout set as 2027 notes redeemed in 2026
feed.summary: Truist Financial (NYSE: TFC) announced it will redeem all outstanding fixed-to-floating senior notes due March 2, 2027, totaling $1.25 billion, on March 2, 2026. The redemption price will be 100% of the principal plus accrued and unpaid interest, with interest ceasing on the redemption date. This move is part of Truist's ongoing management of its funding profile and balance sheet, with total assets reported at $548 billion as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: Synopsys Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Synopsys Inc. (SNPS) saw its stock rise by 4.83% to $442.31 on Wednesday, outperforming the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. This strong performance occurred during a favorable trading session, although the company's shares remain 32.13% below their 52-week high.

feed.title: Johnson Controls signs agreement to acquire Alloy Enterprises, strengthening data center thermal management leadership
feed.summary: Johnson Controls has announced an agreement to acquire Alloy Enterprises, a Boston-based company specializing in advanced thermal management platforms for high-performance data centers. This acquisition will enhance Johnson Controls' leadership in the growing data center cooling segment by integrating Alloy's proprietary direct liquid cooling components, which offer significant improvements in thermal efficiency and reduced energy use. The transaction is expected to be completed in fiscal Q3, subject to regulatory approvals, and aims to accelerate innovation in cooling solutions for critical applications.

feed.title: Host Hotels & Resorts Announces Sale of the Four Seasons Resort Orlando at Walt Disney World® Resort and the Four Seasons Resort and Residences Jackson Hole
feed.summary: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (NASDAQ: HST) has announced the sale of the Four Seasons Resort Orlando at Walt Disney World® Resort and the Four Seasons Resort and Residences Jackson Hole for a combined $1.1 billion. This sale represents an 11.0% unlevered internal rate of return over the company's ownership period and an attractive EBITDA multiple. The company aims to use the proceeds to strengthen its balance sheet and pursue further value-creating opportunities for shareholders.

feed.title: EBay: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: EBay Inc. reported a Q4 profit of $528 million, or $1.15 per share, with adjusted earnings of $1.41 per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The e-commerce company's revenue reached $2.97 billion, also exceeding forecasts. For the upcoming quarter, eBay anticipates per-share earnings between $1.53 and $1.59 and revenue between $3 billion and $3.05 billion.

feed.title: Phillips 66, Citgo seek to buy crude directly from Venezuela - Reuters (PSX:NYSE)
feed.summary: U.S. refiners Phillips 66 and Citgo Petroleum are reportedly looking to purchase heavy crude directly from Venezuela's state oil company, PDVSA, starting in April. This move aims to maximize profits by circumventing intermediary purchases through Chevron or other trading houses. The report suggests a shift in procurement strategy for these refiners.

feed.title: EXEC: KKR Loses Billions in Accell Group’s Debt Restructuring
feed.summary: Accell Group, a Netherlands-based bike manufacturer, announced a restructuring wherein its equity holders, led by private-equity firm KKR, will transfer their shares to senior lenders. This move is in exchange for new funding and significant debt reduction, following a prolonged downturn in the European bike industry and previous debt restructuring efforts. KKR, which acquired Accell in 2022 for €1.56 billion, will lose its investment as the company's financial structure shifts to ensure stability and future growth.

feed.title: CoStar Group Inc. stock rises Wednesday, outperforms market
feed.summary: CoStar Group Inc. (CSGP) shares rose by 7.00% to $48.94 on Wednesday, outpacing the broader market which also saw gains with the S&P 500 Index up 0.56% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.26%. This marks the second consecutive day of gains for the stock.

feed.title: Booking Holdings beats Q4 expectations as room nights rise 9% By Investing.com
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. reported Q4 earnings that significantly exceeded analyst expectations, with adjusted EPS of $48.80 and revenue of $6.35 billion. The company saw a 9% rise in room nights and a 16% increase in gross bookings year-over-year, alongside robust guidance for Q1 and full-year 2026. Additionally, the board approved a 25-to-1 stock split and increased its quarterly cash dividend.

feed.title: CRH raises quarterly dividend by 5% to $0.39 per share
feed.summary: CRH (NYSE:CRH) has announced a 5% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.39 per ordinary share, resulting in a 0.91% dividend yield. The building materials provider, with a market capitalization of $83.62 billion, has consistently paid dividends since 1970. The upcoming dividend is payable on April 8, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 6, 2026.

feed.title: The Hartford Declares Quarterly Dividends Of $0.60 Per Share Of Common Stock And $375 Per Share Of Series G Preferred Stock
feed.summary: The Hartford's Board of Directors has declared quarterly dividends. Common stock shareholders will receive $0.60 per share, payable on April 2 to those on record as of March 2. Additionally, Series G preferred stock shareholders will receive $375 per share (or $0.375 per depository share), payable on May 15 to those on record as of May 1.

feed.title: The Baldwin Group to Participate in the Raymond James Institutional Investor Conference
feed.summary: The Baldwin Group, also known as The Baldwin Insurance Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: BWIN), announced that its CEO, Trevor Baldwin, and CFO, Brad Hale, will present at the Raymond James Institutional Investor Conference on March 2, 2026. A live webcast of the presentation will be available on the company's investor relations website, with a replay also accessible after the event. The company is an independent insurance distribution firm serving over three million clients across the U.S. and internationally.

feed.title: Adamas Trust, Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Adamas Trust, Inc. (Nasdaq: ADAM) announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, highlighting a pivotal year with substantial investment portfolio expansion, increased profitability, and the strategic acquisition of Constructive. The company reported a 44% increase in quarterly EAD, over $100 million in net income, and a 15% dividend raise, while also increasing book value per common share to $9.60. Key developments include significant investments in securities and residential loans, several successful financing activities, and subsequent events in early 2026 showing continued strategic execution.

feed.title: Cytek Biosciences to participate at the TD Cowen 46th Annual Health Care Conference
feed.summary: Cytek Biosciences, Inc. announced its participation in the TD Cowen 46th Annual Health Care Conference. Management will provide a hybrid presentation and fireside chat on March 4th. A live and archived webcast of the event will be available on the company's investors section.

feed.title: Understanding the Setup: (WLDR) and Scalable Risk
feed.summary: This article analyzes Affinity World Leaders Equity Etf (NASDAQ: WLDR), indicating a near-term neutral sentiment despite mid and long-term strength, with a mid-channel oscillation pattern. It highlights an exceptional 12.3:1 risk-reward setup targeting a 3.6% gain versus 0.3% risk. The piece details three AI-generated trading strategies—Position, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging—tailored for various risk profiles and holding periods, alongside multi-timeframe signal analysis.

feed.title: Conagra Brands Announces Appointment of John Mulligan and Pietro Satriano to its Board of Directors
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has appointed John Mulligan, former COO of Target, and Pietro Satriano, former CEO of US Foods Holding Corp., to its board of directors, increasing the board to 12 members. Both bring extensive experience in retail, corporate governance, and the food industry, which Conagra believes will support its strategy, technology integration, and innovation agenda.

feed.title: CNB Bank Joins the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia as a State Member Bank
feed.summary: CNB Bank, a subsidiary of CNB Financial Corporation, announced it has become a state member bank regulated by the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia, effective February 12, 2026. This move is considered an important milestone in the bank's strategic growth, providing a more efficient supervisory process while maintaining regulatory strength. CNB Bank will continue to be regulated by the Pennsylvania Department of Banking and Securities.

feed.title: 1ST SOURCE CORP SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: 1st Source Corporation (SRCE) has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, showcasing robust financial performance with net income of $158.28 million and diluted net income per common share of $6.41. The company highlighted strategic growth in renewable energy and maintained a strong liquidity position, increasing shareholders' equity to 14.08% of total assets. The report also detailed various challenges and risks, including credit, market, climate change, and regulatory issues.

feed.title: Reverse split proposal highlights NYSE bid risk for agilon health (AGL)
feed.summary: agilon health (AGL) is proposing a reverse stock split at a ratio between 1-for-5 and 1-for-25 to address its share price falling below the NYSE's minimum bid requirement of $1.00. The company received a non-compliance notice on November 5, 2025, and believes the split could help it regain compliance and improve marketability to institutional investors. While the board recommends approval, it acknowledges risks such as negative investor perception and no guarantee of sustained price increase or restored liquidity.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (TPL) Texas Pacific Land Corporation Reports Q4 Revenue $211.6M, vs. FactSet Est of $207.5M
feed.summary: Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL) reported Q4 revenue of $211.6 million, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $207.5 million. The company, which specializes in owning and operating land and water resources in Texas, also reported Q4 EPS of $1.79, matching FactSet's estimate. As of late 2024, Texas Pacific Land Corporation managed approximately 3,533 km2 of land in Texas, with revenue primarily derived from land operations and water resources exploitation.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (INVH) Invitation Homes Inc. Reports Q4 Revenue $685.2M, vs. FactSet Est of $685.6M
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. (INVH) reported its Q4 revenue at $685.2 million, falling slightly short of the FactSet estimate of $685.6 million. The article also touches upon their Q4 Adjusted FFO being flat and their expected Adjusted FFO range for 2026. The company owns and manages over 85,000 single-family rental homes.

feed.title: Booking Holdings (BKNG) Releases Q4 2025 Earnings: Revenue Beats but EPS Misses
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (BKNG) reported Q4 2025 revenue of $6.35 billion, exceeding analyst estimates, but its diluted earnings per share of $44.22 missed expectations. Despite the EPS miss, the company showed strong growth in revenue, gross profit, operating profit, and net income, with operating cash flow more than doubling. Insider trading data indicated significant sales by executives, while institutional investors showed mixed activity, and analysts largely maintained "Buy" or "Outperform" ratings with a median price target of $6250.0.

feed.title: Avis Budget shares tumble 14% as EV fleet challenges drive Q4 loss, revenue miss
feed.summary: Avis Budget Group (NASDAQ:CAR) shares fell 14% after the company reported a Q4 revenue miss and a significant loss, largely due to $518 million in impairment charges related to its U.S. electric vehicle fleet. Despite these challenges, the company's CEO, Brian Choi, stated that Avis is focusing on fleet discipline and strengthening its balance sheet for sustainable earnings growth in 2026. The Q4 revenue was $2.7 billion against an expected $2.75 billion, and the company reported a loss of -$21.25 per share.

feed.title: Omnicom Group’s (NYSE:OMC) Q4 CY2025: Beats On Revenue
feed.summary: Omnicom Group (NYSE:OMC) reported Q4 CY2025 results, significantly exceeding revenue expectations with a 27.9% year-on-year increase to $5.53 billion. Despite this, its non-GAAP profit and adjusted EBITDA missed analyst estimates. The company showed strong revenue growth acceleration over the past two years, with analysts projecting continued substantial growth in the next 12 months, though its operating margin and EPS growth trends raise some questions about efficiency.

feed.title: Silver Developer Targets Big Gains with Contango Merger
feed.summary: Dolly Varden Silver Corp. is pursuing a statutory arrangement with Contango ORE Inc., where Contango will acquire all Dolly Varden shares, offering 0.1652 of a Contango share for each Dolly Varden share. The Dolly Varden board unanimously recommends this merger, citing future value for shareholders and a clear path to completion, with shareholders voting on March 17. The company's recent 2025 drilling program at Homestake Ridge yielded "wide, weighty, and hard to ignore" results, described by analyst Jeff Valks, showcasing significant gold and silver mineralization.

feed.title: Dr Reddy's acquires India trademarks for 2 HRT drugs from UK's Mercury Pharma for $32 million
feed.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories is entering the hormone replacement therapy (HRT) segment in India by acquiring trademarks for Progynova and Cyclo-Progynova from Mercury Pharma Group for $32.15 million. This strategic move aims to strengthen Dr Reddy's gynecology portfolio and expand its presence in the HRT market, which addresses symptoms related to estrogen deficiency and menopause. The acquired brands generated sales of Rs 100 crore as per IQVIA MAT December 2025 data.

feed.title: CoStar Group cuts jobs amid AI rollout and Homes.com focus
feed.summary: CoStar Group has confirmed layoffs, attributing them to efficiencies gained from AI adoption and a strategic focus on Homes.com. The company is facing pressure from activist investors to divest Homes.com due to slower-than-expected financial results but remains committed to its long-term growth and digital ecosystem strategy, forecasting profitability for Homes.com by 2030.

feed.title: AMGN: Transformative oncology pipeline advances with T cell engagers, AI, and precision therapies
feed.summary: Amgen (AMGN) is making significant strides in its oncology research and development, focusing on transformative therapies for both solid and hematological cancers. Key advancements include T cell engagers, small molecules, and selective ADCs. The company's existing cancer treatments like IMDELLTRA, LUMAKRAS, and BLINCYTO are being expanded, while AI and biomarker strategies are integrated to drive further innovation and pipeline growth.

feed.title: Adamas Trust Q4 net income rises
feed.summary: Adamas Trust reported an increase in its Q4 net income. This brief article, sourced from Reuters, provides a concise update on the financial performance of Adamas Trust. Further details are accessible on TradingView News.

feed.title: Booking Reports Better Than Expected First Quarter Outlook
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. reported a stronger-than-expected first-quarter outlook, with gross bookings anticipated to rise by 15% at the midpoint, surpassing analyst projections of a 13% increase. The company's revenue outlook also exceeded expectations. This positive forecast suggests continued robust performance within the travel industry.

feed.title: TPL: Record 2025 results with strong royalty and water growth, dividend hike, and major acquisitions
feed.summary: Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL) achieved record revenues, net income, and free cash flow in 2025, driven by strong growth in oil and gas royalty production and water services. The year was marked by major acquisitions, a dividend increase, and a stock split, with ample liquidity available for future investments. This information is based on their SEC 8-K report from February 19, 2026.

feed.title: Shale producer Occidental Petroleum beats quarterly profit estimates
feed.summary: U.S. shale producer Occidental Petroleum surpassed Wall Street's fourth-quarter profit expectations, driven by increased production that counteracted lower crude oil prices. Despite growing concerns about an oil glut and falling Brent crude prices, Occidental's average global production rose to 1.48 million barrels of oil equivalent per day. The company expects capital expenditure for the current year to be between $5.5 billion and $5.9 billion and aims for $2.5 billion in cost savings, while forecasting average annual production of 1.42 MMboepd to 1.48 MMboepd.

feed.title: EBay Reports Quarterly Sales, Profit That Exceeded Estimates
feed.summary: EBay Inc. announced quarterly sales and profit that surpassed analyst estimates. The company's focus on luxury items and refurbished goods is credited with helping it thrive in a competitive e-commerce landscape. For the fourth quarter, eBay reported an adjusted profit of $1.41 per share on $3 billion in revenue, exceeding average estimates of $1.35 per share and $2.87 billion, respectively.

feed.title: BRIEF-ARMOUR Residential REIT Q4 Net Interest Income USD 50.4 Million
feed.summary: ARMOUR Residential REIT reported its Q4 Net Interest Income at USD 50.4 Million. This brief update comes from Reuters via Refinitiv.

feed.title: BeFra Announces Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release Date, Conference Call and Webcast
feed.summary: Betterware de México, S.A.P.I. de C.V. (BeFra) has announced that it will release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 26, 2026, after the U.S. market close. The company will host a conference call and webcast to discuss the results on the same day at 5:30 p.m. (Eastern Time), with replay options available for those unable to participate live. BeFra, also known for its JAFRA acquisition, is a leading direct-to-consumer company in Mexico and the U.S.

feed.title: Geron Corporation Reports Inducement Grants Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
feed.summary: Geron Corporation granted stock options to a new employee for 742,500 shares of common stock at an exercise price of $1.80 per share, effective February 17, 2026. These inducement grants, approved by Geron’s Compensation Committee, vest over four years and were made in accordance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4). Geron is a biopharmaceutical company focused on blood cancer treatments.
